commands.author: yetval

target.title: fix(skills): refuse owner-qualified ClawHub force install over a different owner
target.body:
## Summary
Installing an owner-qualified ClawHub skill with `--force` over a skill already installed under a different owner silently destroys the existing skill and rewrites its tracking to the new owner. With `@alice/weather` installed, running `openclaw skills install @bob/weather --force` reports success while replacing Alice's skill with Bob's under the same local name and flipping the lockfile/origin `ownerHandle` to `bob`. There is no way to keep both and no warning. The CLI itself recommends `--force` when a plain install errors with "already exists", so this is an easy path to data loss of installed state.

This was introduced by the just-landed owner-qualified install support in #94282 (ebd87e7f37).

## Root cause
The update path guards against owner mismatch, but the install path does not. In `resolveRequestedUpdateSlug` the update flow refuses to act when the requested owner differs from the tracked owner:

before (`src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts`, update path only):
```ts
if (trackedOrigin || trackedLockEntry) {
  const trackedOwnerHandle = trackedOrigin?.ownerHandle ?? trackedLockEntry?.ownerHandle;
  if (requestedRef.ownerHandle && trackedOwnerHandle !== requestedRef.ownerHandle) {
    const trackedRef = trackedOwnerHandle ? `@${trackedOwnerHandle}/${trackedSlug}` : trackedSlug;
    throw new Error(
      `Skill "${trackedSlug}" is tracked as ${trackedRef}, not @${requestedRef.ownerHandle}/${trackedSlug}.`,
    );
  }
  return trackedSlug;
}
```

`performClawHubSkillInstall` derives `targetDir` and the lock/origin key from the bare `params.slug` with the owner dropped into `ownerHandle`, and has no equivalent guard. With `--force`, the `pathExists(targetDir)` check is skipped and `installPackageDir` does a backup-then-replace, so an `@bob/weather` install lands directly on top of the tracked `@alice/weather`.

## Fix
Extract the owner-match check into a shared `assertClawHubOwnerMatch` helper used by both the update path and the install path. The install path now reads the existing origin and lockfile entry before overwriting and refuses, even under `--force`, when the requested owner differs from the tracked owner:

after (`src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts`, install path):
```ts
if (params.ownerHandle) {
  const trackedOrigin = await readClawHubSkillOrigin(targetDir);
  const trackedLockEntry = (await readClawHubSkillsLockfile(params.workspaceDir)).skills[
    params.slug
  ];
  if (trackedOrigin || trackedLockEntry) {
    assertClawHubOwnerMatch({
      slug: params.slug,
      requestedOwnerHandle: params.ownerHandle,
      trackedOwnerHandle: trackedOrigin?.ownerHandle ?? trackedLockEntry?.ownerHandle,
    });
  }
}
```

The guard only fires for owner-qualified requests against an already-tracked skill, so fresh installs and same-owner force reinstalls/updates are unaffected. The update flow keeps its identical behavior through the shared helper.

## Why this is the right boundary
The owner-mismatch invariant is one rule that belongs to both lifecycle entry points; the bug was the install path missing the rule the update path already enforced. Sharing one helper removes the asymmetry rather than copying a second guard. The update flow drives install via `installTrackedSkillFromClawHub` with the tracked owner, so requested and tracked owners match and updates still proceed. Bare-slug installs (no `ownerHandle`) keep their prior force-overwrite behavior, matching the update path which also skips the owner check when no owner is requested.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`: 52 passed with the fix; the new test fails on pristine main.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`: clean.
- `oxfmt --check src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`: clean.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` and `-p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`: clean.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: owner-qualified `install --force` over a skill tracked under a different owner must refuse instead of silently downloading over it and rewriting its owner tracking.
Real environment tested: the real `skills install` CLI command action (`registerSkillsCli` -> `installSkillFromClawHub`) run via tsx with no mocks, against the live ClawHub registry, on pristine main@3f0e740f83 and on PR head 75ba27e99b, with a real on-disk workspace (`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`) seeded with an `@alice/weather` install (real origin.json + lock.json at version 1.0.0 and Alice's own SKILL.md).
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<workspace> node --import tsx <cli> skills install @bob/weather --force --global`, with `<workspace>` pre-seeded with an `@alice/weather` install.
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (pristine main 3f0e740f83)
$ openclaw skills install @bob/weather --force --global
Downloading weather@1.0.0 from ClawHub...
Installing to <workspace>/skills/weather...
Installed weather@1.0.0 -> <workspace>/skills/weather
exit code: 0
# lock.json after: weather.ownerHandle = "bob"   (was "alice")
# origin.json after: ownerHandle = "bob", installedAt rewritten
# skills/weather/SKILL.md after: Alice's content replaced by the downloaded skill

# AFTER (this patch, identical inputs, head 75ba27e99b)
$ openclaw skills install @bob/weather --force --global
Skill "weather" is tracked as @alice/weather, not @bob/weather.
exit code: 1
# no "Downloading"/"Installing" lines: refused before any network or backup-replace
# lock.json after: weather.ownerHandle = "alice", version 1.0.0, installedAt 123 (untouched)
# origin.json after: ownerHandle = "alice", installedVersion 1.0.0 (untouched)
# skills/weather/SKILL.md after: Alice's content intact
```
Observed result after fix: on main the live force install reports success and clobbers Alice (download runs, lock/origin owner flips to bob, Alice's SKILL.md is overwritten); with the patch the CLI exits 1 with the tracked-owner error before any download, and Alice's lock, origin, and SKILL.md are all left untouched. The colocated regression test in `src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts` also asserts this and fails on pristine main, with 52/52 passing on the patched tree.
What was not tested: a same-owner force reinstall and a bare-slug force install over a tracked skill (both intentionally still allowed; covered by the colocated unit tests, not re-driven through the live CLI here).

target.title: fix #95295: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95295
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The Web UI sidebar Recent Sessions list could hide existing child-spawned subagent transcripts when the current chat scope was another agent, so users could not intentionally rediscover those sessions from the left sidebar.
- Solution: Keep the existing scoped/default behavior, but add an explicit sidebar **All agents** mode that uses the existing unscoped `sessions.list`/combined-store path and allows subagent/spawned rows in that opt-in view.
- What changed: Added sidebar state and UI for the scope toggle, made only the sidebar toggle request omit scoped `agentId`, kept cron/global/unknown/archived rows excluded, and added controller/rendering regression tests.
- What did NOT change: This does not change the chat picker, default sidebar scoping, shared `loadSessions` explicit `agentId` semantics, gateway protocol schema, stored transcript format, migration behavior, or default visibility of subagent/spawned sessions.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users with persisted child-spawned subagent work can intentionally find and reopen those existing transcripts from the sidebar without losing the safer scoped default view.
- Fixes #95295

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sidebar Recent Sessions can now opt in to cross-agent discovery so an existing row such as `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` with `spawnedBy` can appear when the user enables **All agents**, while default scoped mode still hides those rows.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repository worktree on Windows/Git Bash with Node v22.17.0, pnpm-installed workspace dependencies, and installed Chrome driven by Playwright. The repo engine warning requested Node `>=22.19.0`, but the focused Vitest command and browser proof completed successfully.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; checked `command -v sonarlint || command -v sonar-scanner || command -v sonar-scanner-cli`; started the Control UI E2E Vite server and drove Chrome via Playwright against deterministic Gateway responses; then ran an added local proof that seeded isolated persisted per-agent `sessions.json` stores plus transcript JSONL files, invoked the real `sessions.list` gateway handler for scoped and unscoped requests, and fed those handler responses into the Control UI browser flow.
- **Evidence after fix:** The focused Vitest command exited 0 and covered the sidebar renderer plus session controller path. The first browser proof exited 0 after asserting that the scoped list starts empty with the **All agents** control still reachable, clicking **All agents** renders `Subagent: Planner` and `Main`, excludes the cron row, and sends the sidebar all-agents `sessions.list` request without `agentId` (`includeGlobal`, `includeUnknown`, `configuredAgentsOnly`, `limit: 50`). The stronger session-store/browser proof also exited 0: the real gateway handler returned only `agent:main:main` for `sessions.list` with `agentId: "main"`, returned `agent:orchestrator:subagent:child` plus `agent:main:main` for the unscoped all-agents request, canonicalized the child row's `spawnedBy` to `agent:orchestrator:main`, and the Chrome UI then showed `Subagent: Planner` only after **All agents** was pressed with the final sidebar request omitting `agentId`. The diff whitespace check exited 0. The scanner availability command found no SonarLint/sonar-scanner executable in PATH.
- **Observed result after fix:** The controller regression verifies explicit non-sidebar scoped refreshes still send `agentId`; the renderer regressions verify the All agents control remains reachable with no recent rows and that a cross-agent spawned subagent row is displayed ahead of the main row while a cron row remains excluded; the browser proof validates the same flow through a real Control UI page, WebSocket request path, and visible sidebar DOM; and the session-store proof validates the gateway-side persisted-store split that makes the child-spawned row absent from the scoped main response but present in the unscoped all-agents response.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a long-lived live gateway process in this environment; the added proof invokes the repository's real `sessions.list` gateway handler directly against isolated persisted session stores/transcripts and drives the browser with those exact handler outputs. I could not run SonarLint because no scanner executable was available in PATH.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Addressed within local environment limits — added browser-level Control UI proof and a stronger persisted session-store proof that invokes the real `sessions.list` gateway handler, showing scoped main results omit the orchestrator child while unscoped all-agents results include it with canonical `spawnedBy`; a long-lived live-gateway/Mantis proof remains unavailable in this environment.
- **RF-002:** Fixed — all-agents sidebar refreshes now stay unscoped through row switches and chat/session refresh paths instead of leaving the button pressed while replacing `sessionsResult` with scoped-only data.
- **RF-003:** Addressed within local environment limits — persisted spawned-session stores and transcript JSONL files were seeded locally, the real gateway handler was invoked for scoped and all-agents requests, and Chrome verified the UI reveal path; a maintainer/Mantis-capable long-lived live gateway proof remains unavailable here.
- **RF-004:** Addressed within local environment limits — added browser-level Control UI proof plus persisted-store gateway-handler proof; the remaining gap is a redacted long-lived live-gateway proof with real Mantis/user transcripts, which is unavailable in this environment.
- **RF-005:** Fixed — the **All agents** state is preserved for sidebar-owned refreshes after row selection and chat/gateway refreshes by centralizing sidebar-aware session-list params outside shared `loadSessions`.
- **RF-006:** Maintainer decision needed — sibling candidate PRs exist; this PR intentionally keeps the fix UI-only and same-contract rather than adding protocol/API surface.
- **RF-007:** Maintainer decision needed — the default-safe UX is explicit and default-off, but maintainer confirmation is still appropriate because the opt-in view expands visible session rows across agent scopes.
- **RF-008:** Partially addressed / maintainer review needed — refresh-consistency repair is fixed and browser proof added; live real-gateway proof and canonical product-direction choice remain maintainer-gated.
- **RF-009:** Fixed — added sidebar-aware session-list params so row switches and session refreshes keep **All agents** requests unscoped while explicit non-sidebar `loadSessions(..., { agentId })` remains authoritative. Added a regression for row-switch refresh params.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`, with neighboring focused suites `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/app-gateway.test.ts` run for sidebar/chat/gateway refresh coverage.
- **Scenario locked in:** Explicit non-sidebar `loadSessions(..., { agentId })` requests keep their scoped `agentId`; the sidebar All agents control is reachable with an empty recent list; a cross-agent `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` row with `spawnedBy` renders in Recent Sessions all-agents mode while cron rows remain filtered; and row-switch/session refreshes keep all-agents sidebar requests unscoped.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The controller test guards the request boundary so explicit agent-scoped refreshes cannot be accidentally widened, the helper test guards sidebar-owned refresh persistence, and the render tests guard the sidebar reveal/reachability filters without expanding into gateway/protocol contract changes.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state / security-boundary / compatibility.
- **Risk explanation:** The diff touches session-state UI filtering and a scope control, which can affect whether internal child-spawned transcripts are visible. Compatibility risk is intentionally limited because explicit `loadSessions` agent overrides remain authoritative and no public protocol, schema, config, storage format, or migration contract changes. Related open PR scan found broader approaches for this issue, including PRs that added protocol/API surface; this PR intentionally chooses a narrower same-contract UI path instead.
- **Why acceptable:** The new behavior is default-off, explicitly user-selected, and limited to the sidebar Recent Sessions list. Existing exclusions for archived/global/unknown/cron rows remain. Default scoped behavior, chat picker behavior, and shared scoped refresh behavior are not changed, so the security-boundary/session-state risk is bounded and reviewable.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root cause was a sidebar state/request invariant bug: the Recent Sessions sidebar claimed to be a current-agent view, requested rows with the current agent `agentId`, and then applied a second sidebar filter that rejected subagent-keyed or `spawnedBy` rows. Because both filters lived at the sidebar boundary, already-persisted child-spawned sessions from another agent scope never reached the visible sidebar list.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This fixes the source invariant because the sidebar scope state now owns both sides of the behavior: default mode keeps the scoped `agentId` request and hides spawned/subagent rows, while explicit **All agents** mode keeps sidebar-owned refreshes unscoped and admits spawned/subagent rows in the same sidebar filter. The shared `loadSessions` contract still honors explicit `agentId`, so the patch repairs the sidebar request/filter mismatch rather than masking missing rows after data has already been narrowed.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings for added `return false` guard branches are expected filter predicates, not symptom-masking defaults: they preserve existing exclusions for cron/global/unknown/archived sessions and the default-off subagent/spawned hiding behavior. The public-contract warning is addressed by keeping the canonical protocol/schema unchanged and using the existing unscoped `sessions.list` behavior only from the sidebar toggle request.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary remains the existing `sessions.list` contract and combined session store; this patch does not add schema/protocol/config/data-format changes. The canonical behavior change is confined to the Web UI sidebar state/render/request boundary, with related open PRs considered and broader protocol changes left out of scope.

target.title: fix(doctor): exit non-zero on final invalid config (#77804)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw doctor` printed `Invalid config:` validation errors but still exited `0`. CI/CD pipelines and wrapper scripts that key off doctor's exit code to detect an unhealthy configuration were therefore falsely reassured. This PR sets `process.exitCode = 1` from `runFinalConfigValidationHealth` when the final on-disk config snapshot is still invalid, and makes the `doctor` command wrapper at `src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts` honor `process.exitCode` instead of unconditionally exiting `0`.

Fixes #77804.

The change is small and localized:

- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts` (+3): set `process.exitCode = 1` only if not already non-zero.
- `src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts` (+1/-1): pass `process.exitCode` through to `defaultRuntime.exit(...)`.
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts` (+84): four new vitest cases grafted into main's existing `doctor:final-config-validation` block.
- `src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts` (+19): regression for the command-wrapper exit-code passthrough.

Rebased onto current `main` (was ~12k commits behind). Resolved the test-file conflict by taking main's evolved version and re-grafting the four exit-code cases; dropped the contributor `CHANGELOG.md` entry per repo policy.

## Still-needed validation (against current main)

Confirmed still unfixed upstream: `runFinalConfigValidationHealth` on current `main` reads the final snapshot and prints `Invalid config:` issues but never sets `process.exitCode`, and the `doctor` wrapper still calls `defaultRuntime.exit(0)` unconditionally.

## Real behavior proof

Workspace deps are installed, so I exercised the **real** code path live (no mocks): a real invalid `openclaw.json` on disk, read by the real `readConfigFileSnapshot`, run through the real `doctor:final-config-validation` contribution.

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` returned exit code 0 even after printing `Invalid config:` errors from final validation.
Real environment tested: macOS, node 22.22.2, openclaw source at the rebased-onto-main HEAD. Ran the real `resolveDoctorHealthContributions()` contribution against a real on-disk invalid config via tsx.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: wrote an invalid `openclaw.json`, then ran the real final-config-validation contribution against it with `node --import tsx`.
Evidence after fix: live terminal output below.

```
$ cat $OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH
{"models":{"providers":{"bailian":{"models":[{"compat":{"thinkingFormat":12345}}]}}}}
$ node --import tsx doctor-live-proof.mts
INVALID config -> process.exitCode = 1
first reported errors: ["Invalid config:",
  "- models.providers.bailian.models.0.id: Invalid input: expected string, received undefined",
  "- models.providers.bailian.models.0.name: Invalid input: expected string, received undefined"]
```

Observed result after fix: the real contribution reads the real invalid file via the real `readConfigFileSnapshot`, prints the `Invalid config:` block, and sets `process.exitCode = 1`. With a valid or missing config it leaves `process.exitCode` at 0, and a non-zero code set earlier in the run is never lowered.
What was not tested: a full `openclaw doctor` CLI bootstrap end-to-end (the from-source CLI boot pulls the entire plugin/gateway graph and was impractical to transpile live in this checkout); the focused run exercises the exact code path the CLI uses, and the wrapper passthrough is covered by `register.maintenance.test.ts`.

## Automated tests

Both touched test files pass with deps reconciled:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  40 passed (40)
```

## Risks

Low. Strictly widens the exit-code surface: `doctor` now returns non-zero only on a state the user already sees printed as errors. The wrapper change preserves the existing healthy-run path (when `process.exitCode` is undefined or 0).

No `CHANGELOG.md` entry, per repo policy (release-owned).

target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the JSON embedded-fallback behavior change from this PR; `openclaw agent --json` fallback behavior is back to the existing contract.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2 by replacing the live-update process timing test with deterministic `runExecProcess` regression coverage, so the previous single-line skip branches are gone.
- Final head verified: `8793847f206cca93c49abe33e4f49b9b55a0c4dc`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t 'JSON'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`\n\nChanged-file gate note: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` did not reach pnpm because local `crabbox` failed its binary sanity check. Direct `pnpm check:changed` also could not run in this local checkout because `origin/main...HEAD` has no merge base on the grafted/shallow branch. The focused tests, lint, and diff checks above passed on the final head.

## Process control redaction update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by redacting command-derived process result names for running-session controls (`write`, `submit`, `paste`) plus `kill`, `remove`, `poll`, `log`, `list`, and `send-keys`.
- Added fake-secret regression coverage for `process write` and `process send-keys` command-derived `details.name` values.
- Final head verified locally: `24ac21f4280e9e403722c271d12fda0a4c68535d4`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process-send-keys.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Five coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

> **Refreshed at current head `bf4c277c9b` (supersedes the v6 proof below, which targeted older head `2fc3ecee2d`).**

- **Behavior addressed**: A session takeover during the embedded prompt-lock release window must not silently drop the user's reply, and the injected embedded retry default must refresh with the latest configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` across repeated installs (not freeze the first-captured value).

Please resend your message.") instead of silently dropping the reply; the retry-default wrapper reads the shared live ref at call time so a repeat install refreshes the next call (including downward refresh when the configured value drops); and typecheck is clean at this head.

The CI matrix on this head was still running at proof time.

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally, an incoming steering write changed the transcript without updating the fence, raising `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, which `model-fallback.ts` aborted into a generic failure branch so the user saw no actionable output. After this fix the pure-takeover path returns explicit resend guidance, the SDK retry default is scoped to the embedded prompt-lock window (with configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` taking precedence), and `maxRetries` is forwarded through the native OpenAI transport.

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```
BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) — vitest verbose

      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:


  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.

- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

target.title: fix(cron): block self-narrating auto-announce replies
target.body:
## Summary
- tighten cron auto-delivery instructions so automatic final answers must be the exact user-facing message, not a narration of what was sent
- add a delivery-side guard that blocks compact self-narrating cron auto-announce text before it reaches outbound delivery
- keep substantive user-facing messages and multi-line content deliverable

## Testing
- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts`
- Result: 2 files passed, 45 tests passed

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Cron auto-announce delivery should not send compact self-narrating final text such as a message that says the assistant already sent or asked the user something. It should block that internal/meta narration before outbound delivery and still allow substantive user-facing messages.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at this PR head on macOS, using the repository-installed dependencies and the actual cron isolated-agent delivery dispatch code path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout after applying this patch:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/jeremy-builder/Building/OpenClaw

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  45 passed (45)
Duration  5.20s
```

- Observed result after fix: The delivery regression exercises the cron direct-delivery path with a compact self-narrating final payload and observes `delivered === false`, `deliveryAttempted === true`, and no Telegram send call. The helper regression also observes that a substantive user-facing lunch-options style message is not flagged.
- What was not tested: No live Telegram bot/group end-to-end run was performed for this PR; the proof is from the local OpenClaw runtime test harness and focused dispatch-path regression.

target.title: fix: handle Claude CLI synthetic placeholders
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90789 by recognizing Claude Code CLI synthetic assistant placeholders as CLI-generated no-output metadata instead of treating the turn as an ordinary empty result.

This covers the observed `model: "<synthetic>"` assistant events with:

- `No response requested.`
- `Continue from where you left off.`

## What changed

- Detect Claude stream-json assistant records where `message.model === "<synthetic>"` and the text exactly matches a known Claude CLI placeholder.
- Preserve a `syntheticPlaceholder` marker across both buffered JSONL parsing and streaming parsing, including when a later empty `type: "result"` event arrives.
- Propagate that marker into embedded run metadata as `terminalReplyKind: "synthetic-placeholder"`.
- **Make the classifier path reachable from `runPreparedCliAgent`**: `executeCliAttempt` previously threw `FailoverError(empty_response)` on every empty-text turn, swallowing the synthetic marker before `buildCliRunResult` could surface it. The throw is now skipped when `output.syntheticPlaceholder === true`, so the run result keeps `terminalReplyKind` and the classifier returns the richer `empty_result` verdict.
- Classify synthetic-placeholder terminal replies as fallback-worthy `empty_result` for model fallback / observability, including non-GPT models such as `claude-cli/Claude-Opus-4.7`.
- Keep explicit OpenClaw silent replies (`NO_REPLY`) out of fallback behavior.

## Rationale

The placeholder is generated by the CLI/runtime, not by the assistant and not by OpenClaw's explicit silent-reply contract. Surfacing it as a normal reply would be wrong, but silently swallowing it can leave Telegram/group-chat turns with no visible message and no useful recovery path. Treating it as an empty-result classification preserves observability and allows existing fallback handling to recover.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`
  - 4 files passed, 50 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/cli-output.ts src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts`
  - 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`
  - clean
- `git diff --check`
  - clean

New `src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts` drives `runPreparedCliAgent` end-to-end with a mocked CLI output carrying `syntheticPlaceholder: true`, asserting both that `terminalReplyKind` reaches the result metadata and that `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback` emits the `empty_result` verdict — the integration coverage ClawSweeper P1 asked for.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Claude Code CLI emits a `<synthetic>` assistant placeholder (`No response requested.` / `Continue from where you left off.`) followed by an empty `type: "result"` event when its loop is wedged/timed-out. Before this fix, OpenClaw's `executeCliAttempt` saw the empty text and threw a generic `FailoverError(empty_response)` which left no `terminalReplyKind` metadata and no synthetic-placeholder signal for the model-fallback classifier, so the affected Telegram turn went silent with no recovery path (issue #90789).
- **Real environment tested**: local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/wanglu241/code/openclaw`, branch `fix/90789-claude-cli-synthetic-placeholder` (head `8d6f1a7a`), node v26.0.0, pnpm/tsx 4.22.3, macOS 25.4.0 arm64. Parser + classifier executed against the real source via `pnpm exec tsx`, with the exact JSONL byte sequence the CLI emits (taken from `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts`'s captured fixture).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Build a one-off harness (`scratch/synthetic-placeholder-proof.ts`) that calls `parseCliJsonl(...)` with the synthetic-placeholder stream-json sequence and then invokes `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback(...)` against the resulting run-result shape.
  2. Check out the upstream/main versions of `src/agents/cli-output.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts`; run the harness; record output.
  3. Restore the PR head versions of those files; run the harness again; record output.
  4. Restore working tree, run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts` to drive the same flow through `runPreparedCliAgent` end-to-end.
- **Evidence after fix:** redacted terminal output from `pnpm exec tsx scratch/synthetic-placeholder-proof.ts`:

```text
=== BASELINE: upstream/main (no synthetic placeholder handling) ===
---- parser output ----
{
  "text": "",
  "sessionId": "redacted-session",
  "usage": {
    "input": 8
  }
}
---- classifier verdict ----
null

=== CANDIDATE: PR fix/90789-claude-cli-synthetic-placeholder ===
---- parser output ----
{
  "text": "",
  "sessionId": "redacted-session",
  "usage": {
    "input": 8
  },
  "syntheticPlaceholder": true
}
---- classifier verdict ----
{
  "message": "claude-cli/Claude-Opus-4.7 ended with a synthetic placeholder and no visible assistant reply",
  "reason": "format",
  "code": "empty_result"
}
```

End-to-end `runPreparedCliAgent` integration test live output (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`):

```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/wanglu241/code/openclaw

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts (2 tests) 3.04s
   ✓ runPreparedCliAgent synthetic-placeholder propagation > marks runs with synthetic-placeholder terminal reply kind and triggers empty_result fallback
   ✓ runPreparedCliAgent synthetic-placeholder propagation > does not mark ordinary CLI runs and leaves the classifier null

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: with the PR applied, the JSONL parser surfaces `syntheticPlaceholder: true` (baseline does not), the run result carries `terminalReplyKind: "synthetic-placeholder"` (baseline strips it), and the model-fallback classifier returns `{ reason: "format", code: "empty_result", message: "...synthetic placeholder..." }` (baseline returns `null`). Downstream model-fallback machinery therefore now sees a fallback-worthy outcome instead of a silent turn for the wedge-aborted Claude CLI case from #90789.
- **What was not tested**: Native Telegram Desktop capture under Crabbox could not exercise this code path because the bundled `extensions/qa-lab` mock provider does not (yet) emit `<synthetic>` stream-json events to OpenClaw — that is why the workflow's `Run agentic native Telegram proof` job posted a `comparison.status: skipped, pass: false` capture-infrastructure manifest. Adding mock-provider support for synthetic-placeholder injection is tracked as an out-of-scope follow-up; the live tsx run above and the new `runPreparedCliAgent` integration test cover the same code path end-to-end at the runtime layer.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups

- Teach `extensions/qa-lab` mock providers to optionally emit Claude CLI `<synthetic>` placeholder stream-json so the Mantis Telegram Desktop scenario can produce a native before/after recording for this fix.

target.title: feat(Signal): support note-to-self ingress
target.body:
## Summary

### What problem does this PR solve?

- Adds explicit Signal `ingressMode: "note-to-self"` support so linked-device `signal-cli` setups can use the operator's Signal Note to Self chat as the OpenClaw conversation surface.
- Removes the practical need to buy, register, and maintain a second phone number and Signal account just to run OpenClaw as a personal Signal bot.
- Promotes only self-addressed `syncMessage.sentMessage` envelopes into the normal inbound pipeline, while continuing to drop sent transcripts addressed to other people and direct own-account non-sync echoes.
- Adds self-reply echo suppression so OpenClaw replies sent into Note to Self do not re-enter as fresh prompts.

### Why does this matter now?

- Today, a reliable Signal setup effectively assumes a dedicated bot number/account. That is a high-friction requirement for the common personal use case where the operator only wants to message their own OpenClaw instance through Signal, not expose OpenClaw to contacts.
- Signal Note to Self is the natural secure chat surface for that use case, and linked-device Note to Self messages arrive as sent-sync events that need explicit, self-only handling before agent dispatch.
- This PR makes that behavior explicit and covered across opt-in config, UUID handling, attachments, edits, approval reactions, agent reaction actions, and self-reply loop protection.

### What is the intended outcome?

- Existing `standard` Signal deployments keep their current behavior.
- Users who opt into `channels.signal.ingressMode="note-to-self"` can send text, attachments, edits, and approval reactions through their own Signal Note to Self chat.
- OpenClaw ignores sent-sync transcripts to other Signal recipients and avoids looping on its own Note to Self replies.
- After community testing and follow-up hardening, this mode could become the default-recommended Signal gateway setup for personal deployments in a future PR.

### What is intentionally out of scope?

- No global/core `dmPolicy` changes.
- No change to the default-recommended Signal setup in this PR.
- No required `accountUuid` config for normal users; UUID discovery reads the local signal-cli account store instead.
- No durable persistence for Note to Self self-reply echo markers across gateway restarts; replay suppression is intentionally process-local.

### What does success look like?

- Focused Signal tests pass for account discovery, Note to Self sync promotion, UUID-only routing, edits, attachment-only messages, approval reactions, echo suppression, and legacy approval-reaction key compatibility.
- A real linked-device Signal setup can process phone-originated Note to Self text, image-only, edited-message, approval-reaction, and agent-driven reaction action flows without observed self-reply loops.

### What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `ingressMode="note-to-self"` is bounded to Signal and remains opt-in.
- Whether self-addressed sent-sync promotion cannot accidentally process transcripts to other recipients.
- Whether echo suppression is strong enough without adding persistent state.
- Whether UUID normalization/discovery and approval reaction matching preserve existing behavior.

## Linked context

### Which issue does this close?

Closes #75870.

### Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Supersedes and extends #75890.

Related #5722.

### Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request cited; this follows the linked issue and earlier PR context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Linked-device Signal deployments can route user-authored Note to Self messages into OpenClaw. The same mode must keep ignoring sent transcripts to other contacts and must not loop when OpenClaw's own replies or reactions sync back through Signal.
- Real environment tested: Focused local Vitest coverage and branch autoreview ran in the OpenClaw checkout. Live proof ran against a real linked-device Signal setup using `signal-cli 0.14.3`, an installed local OpenClaw build from this branch, and `channels.signal.ingressMode="note-to-self"` with `receiveMode="on-start"`, `dmPolicy="disabled"`, and `groupPolicy="disabled"`. Agent-driven Signal reaction-action proof also used `tools.profile="messaging"`, because the `coding` profile intentionally removes the `message` tool.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Local verification commands were `pnpm test extensions/signal/src/account-store.test.ts extensions/signal/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/signal/src/message-actions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.tool-result.autostart.test.ts extensions/signal/src/normalize.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts extensions/signal/src/self-reply-echoes.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`, `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`, `pnpm tsgo:prod`, `pnpm check:test-types`, `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`, `pnpm config:channels:check`, `git diff --check`, and `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`. Packaging and install proof was `pnpm build`, `npm pack`, install the packed local build, `openclaw gateway restart`, `openclaw status --deep`, and `openclaw logs --limit 80 --plain`. Live Signal proof covered these scenarios: phone-originated Note to Self text prompt requesting a unique reply token; phone-originated attachment-only Note to Self image prompt; edited Note to Self prompt changing the requested reply token; `/diagnostics note-to-self approval reaction test` approved by reacting `👍` in Signal; installed local build retested with a fresh Note to Self text prompt; inbound normal DM from another Signal account to the configured Signal number; outbound normal DM from the configured Signal account to another Signal account; direct CLI reaction delivery with `openclaw message react --channel signal --target <redacted-own-signal-account> --message-id <redacted-message-id> --emoji "👀" --json`; agent-driven `message` tool reaction add after `openclaw config set tools.profile messaging` and `openclaw gateway restart`; agent-driven `message` tool reaction add for `✅`; agent-driven `message` tool reaction removal for `✅`.
- Evidence: Unit and integration tests cover Note to Self sent-sync promotion, third-party sent transcript drops, UUID-only paths, edits, attachment-only messages, approval reactions, agent reaction actions, account UUID discovery, authoritative configured signal-cli `dataDir` handling, outbound echo recording, timestamp-less and timestamped media fallback suppression, reaction echo suppression, reaction response-loss ambiguity handling, default-account UUID inheritance/deletion, UUID-preserving allowlists, and legacy approval-reaction key compatibility. Live proof showed Signal channel startup and reply delivery in the gateway log:

```text
channels/signal [default] starting provider (http://127.0.0.1:8080)
signal-cli: INFO HttpServerHandler - Started HTTP server on /127.0.0.1:8080
channels/signal delivered reply to <redacted-own-signal-account>
```

- Observed result: Local verification passed: the focused Signal suite passed with 156 tests, channel config check passed, bundled-extension lint passed, production type checks passed, test type checks passed, package-boundary compile passed, whitespace checks passed, and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings. Gateway proof passed: `openclaw status --deep` reported `Signal ON OK configured` on the installed local build. Note to Self text proof passed: a phone-originated Note to Self prompt produced the requested unique token. Note to Self media proof passed: an attachment-only image prompt reached a vision-capable model and the reply described the image text. Note to Self edit proof passed: editing the original Signal message produced a second reply with the edited requested token. Approval reaction proof passed: reacting `👍` to the diagnostics approval prompt resolved the exec approval exactly once with `Exec approval allowed once. Resolved by Signal approval (<redacted-own-signal-account>).` Third-party inbound DM isolation passed: a normal DM from another Signal account arrived on the configured Signal account's phone and linked signal-cli instance, but OpenClaw did not reply, did not send a pairing request, and did not appear to start an agent turn. Third-party outbound sent-sync isolation passed: a normal DM sent from the configured Signal account to another Signal account appeared in signal-cli and on both phones as a regular Signal message, but OpenClaw did not reply or otherwise act on that sent-sync transcript. Direct reaction delivery passed: CLI reaction delivery returned `handledBy: "plugin"`, `ok: true`, and `added: "👀"`. Agent-driven reaction proof passed: with `tools.profile="messaging"`, the agent used the `message` tool to add `👀`, used the `message` tool to add `✅`, then used the `message` tool to remove `✅`. No self-reply loop or reaction-triggered loop was observed in any live proof path.
- What was not tested: Broader Signal device/version coverage is left to CI and maintainer/community testing. Agent-driven reaction actions were live-tested only after enabling the `messaging` tool profile; with `tools.profile="coding"`, the `message` tool is intentionally unavailable and the model replied with text instead of sending a reaction. A daemon-originated self-send was tested and confirmed not to emit a receive event on `/api/v1/events`; the successful Note to Self live proof used the primary phone app, which is the real user path.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Live Signal proof used one local linked-device Signal setup with redacted account identifiers. It proves the intended personal Note to Self path and the tested isolation cases, not every Signal client/device version.

## Tests and validation

### Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test extensions/signal/src/account-store.test.ts extensions/signal/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/signal/src/message-actions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.tool-result.autostart.test.ts extensions/signal/src/normalize.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts extensions/signal/src/self-reply-echoes.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

### What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Account UUID discovery from signal-cli config/account-store layouts.
- Authoritative configured signal-cli `dataDir` handling, including stale and ambiguous configured stores that fail closed instead of falling back to a different default profile.
- Parse-compatible `accountUuid` handling for shipped string configs, with runtime UUID normalization for bare, compact, and `uuid:<id>` forms.
- Note to Self sync-message promotion for text, nested messages, edits, and attachment-only messages.
- Third-party sent transcript drops for phone and UUID destination mismatches.
- Self-reply echo suppression for timestamp IDs, timestamp-less text/media fallbacks, timestamped media exact/generic fallbacks, provisional pre-send guards, reaction echoes, ambiguous response loss, and late UUID discovery.
- Signal approval reaction matching across phone, compact UUID, hyphenated UUID, and legacy persisted reaction keys.
- Default/named account inheritance behavior for Note to Self mode and `accountUuid`.
- Agent-driven Signal reaction actions, including forwarding resolved account, UUID, and signal-cli config path context into reaction sends.
- UUID-preserving Signal allowlist/security normalization for `uuid:<id>` entries.

### What behavior does this coverage protect?

- Linked-device Note to Self prompts arrive as `syncMessage.sentMessage`, so current behavior needs a self-only path into agent dispatch.
- Note to Self mode needs coverage for UUID identity, attachments, edits, approval reactions, agent reaction actions, third-party transcript drops, and self-reply echoes.

### If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; focused regression coverage was added and live behavior was verified.

## Risk checklist

### Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Signal users can opt into a new `note-to-self` ingress mode.

### Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This adds `channels.signal.ingressMode` and documents optional `channels.signal.accountUuid`, plus generated channel config metadata and docs. Existing configs keep the default `standard` mode; `accountUuid` remains parse-compatible with shipped string configs and only valid UUID forms are used for runtime identity matching.

### Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, bounded to Signal channel ingress and native approval reaction handling. There are no new external network calls or provider/tool permissions.

### What is the highest-risk area?

- Accidentally treating sent transcripts to other Signal recipients as user prompts, or letting OpenClaw's own Note to Self replies loop back into the agent.

### How is that risk mitigated?

- The mode is explicit opt-in and Signal-local.
- Self-addressed sent-sync promotion checks source/destination identity, including UUID-only envelopes and valid runtime UUID normalization.
- Sent transcripts to other recipients are dropped before dispatch.
- Self-reply echo suppression records outbound replies, media fallbacks, and reaction fingerprints in process memory under the effective Signal phone/UUID identities.
- Focused tests cover destination mismatch drops, UUID normalization, edits, attachments, approval reactions, agent reaction actions, and echo suppression.

## Current review state

### What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer review.

### What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Nothing known from the author side. CI and maintainer review are still needed after PR creation.

### Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local branch autoreview reports no accepted/actionable findings. ClawSweeper's `accountUuid` compatibility review is addressed by permissive schema parsing with runtime UUID normalization.

## Notes For Maintainers

- This intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`; release-owned changelog generation should pick up the PR/commit context.
- `channels.signal.accountUuid` intentionally remains parse-compatible with shipped string configs. Runtime identity matching only treats valid bare, compact, or legacy `uuid:<id>` UUID forms as UUIDs; docs recommend bare UUIDs and normal users should rely on auto-discovery instead.
- If a maintainer wants live Signal proof before merge, the code is structured so the requested scenario can be demonstrated without changing the PR: configure `ingressMode: "note-to-self"`, start the Signal monitor, and send a Note to Self message from the primary device.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix**: Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

PR surface: Source +244, Tests +230. Total +474 across 8 files.

target.title: fix(memory): honor qmd search timeout for memory_search
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`memory_search` had a fixed 15s outer deadline, but QMD-backed searches can legitimately take longer when `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` is raised. The old outer guard could time out the tool before QMD finished a configured search.

Fixes #91947.

## Why This Change Was Made

QMD search has a longer lifecycle than a single command timeout: direct multi-collection probes, search/query/vsearch attempts, unsupported-mode fallback, missing-collection repair, pending update wait, optional dirty sync, and timer grace can all be part of one user-visible search.

This PR keeps the timeout decision on the memory manager instead of mapping one QMD command timeout onto the whole tool. It also intentionally does not change QMD subprocess cancellation or `runCliCommand`; that remains separate from #93394.

## User Impact

QMD-backed `memory_search` can now complete when its configured QMD search timeout is intentionally raised above the old 15s guard.

Default/short-timeout QMD users keep fast failure behavior: if the configured QMD search command timeout is 15s or less, `memory_search` stays on the old 15s guard. When QMD does opt into a longer whole-search budget, the initial search, zero-hit forced sync, and retry share one manager deadline instead of each getting a fresh long timeout.

Non-QMD behavior stays bounded by the default guard: runtime loading, manager setup, wiki supplements, builtin searches, and builtin forced-sync retry share the existing 15s deadline. If QMD fails and the wrapper falls back to the builtin index during the same call, that fallback search has its own 15s cap instead of inheriting the longer QMD budget.

No storage migration or data model change is required.

## Evidence

AI-assisted: yes.

Real QMD-backed behavior proof on this PR head:

- Harness used the repo-local `./node_modules/.bin/tsx --conditions=development` entrypoint.
- Created a temporary workspace with `MEMORY.md` and `memory/qmd-proof.md`.
- Configured `memory.backend = "qmd"`, `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs = 60000`, `searchMode = "search"`, and an explicit allow scope for the standalone harness.
- Wrapped the live `npx -y @tobilu/qmd` CLI with a script that sleeps 16s only for `search|query|vsearch` commands.

```json
{
```
